titleStmt.title: Letter from Lancelot Worthy Horne to Command Paymaster, 8 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Lancelot Worthy Horne

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Lancelot Worthy Horne (1875-1924), railway superintendent, to the command paymaster. The letter regards a journey made by a party, including Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), former chief secretary for Ireland. Horne states that many in the party did not hold valid tickets and that he was informed to apply for the payment of £14.17.6.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5612/22940

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
L.W.Horne Superintendent of the Line London & North Western Railway, Office of Superintendent of the Line, Euston Station, London N.W. In your reply please refer to 85/6516W May 6th 1916. Dear Sir, We made special arrangements at the
request of the Government for the journey of
the Rt. Hon. A. Birrell and party from London
to Holyhead on Wednesday, April 26th. Some of the passengers held tickets, but seven
had neither ticket nor warrant for same from
Euston to Holyhead, and one passenger held
a ticket from Euston to Chester only. We were requested by Captain Butler
to apply for payment, and I shall be glad if
you will kindly send me a warrant to cover
the fares for seven first class passengers
Euston to Holyhead (Â£14,17.6) and one first
class passenger, Chester to Holyhead (15/-),
and oblige. Yours faithfully L.W. Home
per L.W.H The Command Paymaster Irish Command, DUBLIN
